L. 106–387, § 1(a) [title IX, § 911]114 Stat. 1549Oct. 28, 2000“(a).—In GeneralExcept as provided in subsection (b), this title [enacting this chapter] shall take effect on the date of enactment of this Act [], and shall apply thereafter in any fiscal year.Oct. 28, 2000“(b).—Existing SanctionsIn the case of any unilateral agricultural sanction or unilateral medical sanction that is in effect as of the date of enactment of this Act, this title shall take effect 120 days after the date of enactment of this Act, and shall apply thereafter in any fiscal year.”

children.heading: Agricultural commodity
children.label: 1 Agricultural commodity
children.text: The term “agricultural commodity” has the meaning given the term in section 102 of the Agricultural Trade Act of 1978 ().7 U.S.C. 5602

children.label: A
children.text: any program administered under the Food for Peace Act ( et seq.);7 U.S.C. 1691

children.label: B
children.text: any program administered under ;section 1431 of title 7

children.label: C
children.text: any program administered under the Agricultural Trade Act of 1978 ( et seq.);7 U.S.C. 5601

children.label: D
children.text: any commercial export sale of agricultural commodities; or

children.label: E
children.text: any export financing (including credits or credit guarantees) provided by the United States Government for agricultural commodities.

children.label: A
children.text: in the case of , only a joint resolution introduced within 10 session days of Congress after the date on which the report of the President under is received by Congress, the matter after the resolving clause of which is as follows: “That Congress approves the report of the President pursuant to section 903(a)(1) of the Trade Sanctions Reform and Export Enhancement Act of 2000, transmitted on _______.”, with the blank completed with the appropriate date; andsection 7202(a)(1) of this titlesection 7202(a)(1) of this title

children.label: B
children.text: in the case of section 7205(1) of this title, only a joint resolution introduced within 10 session days of Congress after the date on which the report of the President under section 7205(2) of this title is received by Congress, the matter after the resolving clause of which is as follows: “That Congress approves the report of the President pursuant to section 906(1) of the Trade Sanctions Reform and Export Enhancement Act of 2000, transmitted on _______.”, with the blank completed with the appropriate date.11So in original. Probably should be section “7204”.1

children.heading: Medical device
children.label: 4 Medical device
children.text: The term “medical device” has the meaning given the term “device” in .section 321 of title 21

children.heading: Medicine
children.label: 5 Medicine
children.text: The term “medicine” has the meaning given the term “drug” in .section 321 of title 21

children.label: A
children.text: a multilateral regime and the other member countries of that regime have agreed to impose substantially equivalent measures; or

children.label: B
children.text: a mandatory decision of the United Nations Security Council.

children.label: A
children.text: a multilateral regime and the other member countries of that regime have agreed to impose substantially equivalent measures; or

children.label: B
children.text: a mandatory decision of the United Nations Security Council.
